channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Buhle Ngaba’s BLING! returns to Market Theatre, Venom & Shishiliza drop new single, and Content Creator Awards open entries
item.description: Buhle Ngaba brings her acclaimed production BLING! back to The Market Theatre Laboratory in Johannesburg, where it was first developed during the 2024 Barney Simon Residency. Following sold-out perfor...

item.title: Stephen Colbert's Late Show finale features iconic guests including Paul McCartney
item.description: The finale of Stephen Colbert’s Late Show was a highly anticipated event, featuring an impressive roster of celebrity guests. Among those confirmed to appear were legendary musician Paul McCartney, ac...

item.title: A Glimpse of Love Amid the Spanish Civil War: The Wedding of Alfonsa and Ernesto
item.description: On October 18, 1936, amid the Siege of Madrid during the Spanish Civil War, Alfonsa and Ernesto, two Republican milicianos, decided to marry. Their union, captured by renowned Spanish photojournalist ...

item.title: Trump delays executive order on AI regulation after pressure from advisors and tech leaders
item.description: President Donald Trump was reportedly set to issue an executive order aimed at regulating artificial intelligence but abruptly reversed his decision following pushback from key advisors and influentia...

item.title: The 1955 Maserati A6G/54 2000 Spyder Zagato: A Classic Italian Sports Car
item.description: The 1955 Maserati A6G/54 2000 Spyder Zagato stands out as one of the rarest and most elegant Italian sports cars from the 1950s. Built on Maserati’s lightweight tubular chassis, it features a 2.0-lite...

item.title: Volunteers Help Deliver Essential Supplies to Remote Northern Canadian Communities
item.description: The North of 60 Project, led by The Society of St. Vincent de Paul in St. Albert, continues to provide vital support to residents of Canada’s northern communities. One notable shipment is destined for...

item.title: Punjab Records Highest Number of Custodial Torture Complaints, FIA Reports
item.description: According to a report submitted to the Lahore High Court (LHC), Punjab is leading the country in complaints related to custodial torture, with 266 inquiries out of 364 registered nationwide under the ...

item.title: Prince William joins Aston Villa fans to celebrate Europa League triumph
item.description: Prince William, the Prince of Wales, celebrated Aston Villa's Europa League victory with unbridled enthusiasm, sharing moments of joy with fellow supporters. Attending the final in Istanbul, William e...

item.title: Details of Tickets and Seating Arrangement for 'F Forever 1st World Tour' in Manila
item.description: Fans of F4 have a reason to be excited as the popular Taiwanese boy band announces their 'F Forever 1st World Tour' in Manila. The concert, featuring iconic members Jerry Yan, Van Ness Wu, Vic Chou, a...

item.title: Prof. Faith Ken-Aminikpo Highlights Importance of Theatre in Modern Education at UNIPORT

item.title: Michael Ramirez Reflects on Memorial Day Through Cartoon Commentary
item.description: On May 21, 2026, political cartoonist Michael Ramirez published a piece reflecting on Memorial Day. The cartoon, which appears in The Washington Post's Opinion section, serves as a reminder of the sac...

item.title: Air France Flight Diverted to Canada Due to Suspected Ebola Case
item.description: An Air France flight heading from Paris to the United States was redirected to Canada after U.S. authorities denied it entry into American airspace because of health concerns about a potential Ebola c...

item.title: Rise ’76: Revisiting the Legacy and Memories of the 1976 Soweto Uprising
item.description: Rise ’76, created by Tiisetso Mashifane wa Noni, is a compelling theatre production reflecting on the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Soweto Youth Uprising. Unlike performances that aim for instant emoti...

item.title: Supreme Judicial Council Rejects Complaints Against Chief Justice Afridi
item.description: ISLAMABAD: On Thursday, Chief Justice of Pakistan Yahya Afridi disclosed the decisions of the Supreme Judicial Council (SJC) made on May 14, which rejected five separate complaints filed against him b...

item.title: UK sees record rise in melanoma skin cancer cases
item.description: The UK has recorded over 20,000 new cases of melanoma, the deadliest form of skin cancer, for the first time, according to Cancer Research UK. Excessive exposure to ultraviolet (UV) light from the sun...

item.title: Port of Dover warns of delays as new EU border system begins during half-term travel
item.description: The Port of Dover has advised ferry passengers to anticipate delays during the half-term holiday period, as the European Union's new Entry Exit System (EES) has now been implemented. This system, oper...

item.title: Trump Administration Eases Limits on Hydrofluorocarbons Despite Environmental Concerns
item.description: The Trump administration, through the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) led by Lee Zeldin, announced a rollback of restrictions on hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), chemicals widely used in air condition...

item.title: Ikwerre Monarchs Gather to Discuss Community Development and Security

item.title: Lack of African Language Support Limits Participation on Social Media
item.description: Research show that most social media platforms still fail to support the many languages spoken across Africa. Even though Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram now offer some African languages, majority of...

item.title: LAUSD Plans Major Layoffs and Budget Cuts Amid Declining Enrollment
item.description: The Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) is preparing to lay off over 1,000 employees following a recent Board of Education vote and additional terminations of staff without union protections. ...
